title: A systematic review and meta-analysis of acceptance- and mindfulness-based interventions for DSM-5 anxiety disorders
headline: A systematic review and meta-analysis of acceptance- and mindfulness-based interventions for DSM-5 anxiety dis
summary: A meta-analysis / systematic review of Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR) examined emotional wellbeing. We found 23 studies, mostly of unclear risk of bias, including 1815 adults with different DSM-5 anxiety disorders. The evidence suggests short-term anxiolytic effects of acceptance- and mindfulness-based interventions.
keyFindings: This meta-analysis systematically reviewed the evidence on standardized acceptance-/mindfulness-based interventions in DSM-5 anxiety disorders.
We found 23 studies, mostly of unclear risk of bias, including 1815 adults with different DSM-5 anxiety disorders.
The evidence suggests short-term anxiolytic effects of acceptance- and mindfulness-based interventions.
limitations: Risk of bias was assessed using the Cochrane tool.
Several of the pooled studies had methodological weaknesses, which lowers confidence in the result.
The evidence is limited or mixed, so firm conclusions are not yet warranted.
Pooled estimates combine heterogeneous studies; effect sizes vary by practice, population, and control condition.
simplifiedTitle: A systematic review and meta-analysis of acceptance- and mindfulness-based interventions for DSM-5 anxiety dis
aiSnippet: Meta-analysis / Systematic review, Scientific reports (2021). This meta-analysis systematically reviewed the evidence on standardized acceptance-/mindfulness-based interventions in DSM-5 anxiety disorders. We found 23 studies, mostly of unclear risk of bias, including 1815 adults with different DSM-5 anxiety disorders. The evidence suggests short-term anxiolytic effects of acceptance- and mindfulness-based interventions.
whyItMatters: MoM shares directed attention (the focused-attention component) in a brief, guided format with the practice studied here. Because the benefit is linked to that mechanism rather than to any one method, it's reasonable to expect it may extend to MoM as well — though MoM hasn't been studied directly for this outcome.
